info.title: Starmind
info.description: The Starmind Connector enables seamless integration with Starmind's knowledge engine, allowing users to access verified answers and expert insights across their organisation. By connecting to Starmind, your workflows and agents can intelligently retrieve responses to business-critical questions, surface expert knowledge, and streamline decision-making processes.

contact.name: Starmind technical support experts are ready to listen and help

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of returned questions. Defaults to 10.
parameters.x-ms-summary: Maximum number of questions to return

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter the list of questions using a predefined filter.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Specify the criteria the result is sorted by

get.description: Finds questions based on the provided query parameters.

description.description: Question description providing detailed context and information about the question

language.description: The question language will be detected automatically based on the title and description text. Providing a language explicitly is usually not necessary, and only helps in cases where the automatic language detection is not able to detect the language unambiguously

knowledge_space.description: the id of the knowledge space to assign the question to

items.description: Paginated results of the question search.

total.description: Total number of questions matching the request.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit the number of returned questions. Defaults to 10.
parameters.x-ms-summary: Maximum number of questions to return

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter the list of questions using a predefined filter.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Specify the criteria the result is sorted by

get.description: Searches questions using optional `query`, `limit` (default 10), `filter`, and `sort`; returns a paginated collection with question `items`. By default, all published questions are returned, ordered by their last activity (descending). A combination of filters, search queries and ordering criteria can be applied to the result.

post.description: Creates a new question draft. The draft is not yet searchable or answerable by others. After creation, publish the draft (via the publish endpoint) to trigger expert search, make the question discoverable, and allow other users to answer it. Providing the optional description helps experts understand the question better and increases the chances of getting high-quality answers.

category.description: The optional id of the category to assign the question draft to

title.description: Question title, Between 4 and 100 characters by default. Depending on the network configuration the title max length may be limited or extended further.
title.example: How to integrate with the Starmind API?

description.description: The optional question description providing detailed context and information about the question. Depending on the network configuration the description might be enforced to be non-empty.
description.example: I am looking for information on how to integrate with the Starmind API. Specifically, I need details on authentication, available endpoints, and best practices for making requests.

knowledge_space.description: the id of the knowledge space to assign the question to

200.description: Created question draft for further processing such as publishing.
The number of experts notified for the published question

parameters.name: questionId
parameters.x-ms-summary: ID of the question to publish
parameters.description: The ID of the question draft to be published

200.description: The number of experts notified for the published question

put.description: Publishes a question draft. Publishing the question triggers expert search, makes the question discoverable, and allows other users to answer it. Questions are always published anonymously.

parameters.name: questionId
parameters.x-ms-summary: ID of the question to publish
parameters.description: The ID of the question draft to be published

get.description: Retrieves the complete details for a specific question, including solutions, comments, and concepts.

parameters.name: questionId
parameters.description: The ID of the question to retrieve

concept_scores.description: Expert search concept matches including scores

label_matches.description: The matched labels for the expert search query

post.description: Finds experts based on the provided context

text_query.description: The text query to search for experts. This can be a question or a topic of interest.

score.description: Relevance score indicating how well the expert matches the query

experts.description: List of experts matching the search query. May be empty if no experts are found.

label_matches.description: The matched labels for the expert search query. May be empty if no labels match.

post.description: Finds experts based on the provided text_query. The search leverages the knowledge graph to identify users with relevant expertise and knowledge related to the query. Provide the optional language to specify the preferred language for concept label filtering. This does not affect the search matching itself; it only filters which expertise we show from returned users by selecting concepts based on the primary label in the chosen language. Returned concepts are deduplicated when multiple concepts share the same primary label.

text_query.description: Primary search text to identify experts. Can be a question, a summary of a text, one or multiple topics.
text_query.example: Who knows about Docker and Starmind APIs?

200.description: Returns the user object for the specified globalUserId.

post.description: Resolves the globalUserId to user object

query.example: query getUser($globalUserId: UUID!) { user(globalUserId: $globalUserId) { globalUserId firstname lastname email languageId avatarUrl company department position location about countryId created dateDeleted isFederated } }

get.description: Retrieves user information based on the provided user id.

200.description: Successful response with the requested user wrapped in a GraphQL-style envelope (data.user); if not found, data.user is null. An optional errors array may be present.

user.about: Experienced software developer with a passion for knowledge sharing.

knowledge_space_id.description: The unique knowledge space id to which the question belongs.

upvote_count.description: The total number of upvotes the question has received.

view_count.description: The number of times the question has been viewed by users.

published.description: Timestamp indicating when the question was published (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

is_deleted.description: Indicates if the question is deleted. Deleted questions are not visible to users, but can still be accessed by the administrators.

last_activity.description: Timestamp indicating the last activity with the question (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

is_published.description: The question is_published flag indicates whether the question is published or not. A published solution is visible to all users, while an unpublished solution is in draft state only visible to the author.

created.description: Timestamp indicating when the question was created (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

description.description: Question description providing detailed context and information about the question

solution_count.description: This is the number of solutions that have been provided for this question.

content_updated.description: Timestamp indicating when the question's content or attachments were last updated (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

updated.description: Last time the resource has been updated in the DB. This date does not reflect content updates but any update to any fields of the record. Use content_updated for information about content updates.

label_match.description: The label that matched the concept

ontology_id.description: The unique ontology id to which the concept belongs

created.description: Timestamp indicating when the attachment was created (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

link.description: The relative url to the attachment resource

content_updated.description: Timestamp indicating when the comment's description was last updated (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

created.description: Timestamp indicating when the comment was created (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

description.description: This is the main text of the comment that provides additional information or context to the question or solution.

updated.description: Last time the resource has been updated in the DB. This date does not reflect content updates but any update to any fields of the record. Use content_updated for information about content updates.

upvote_count.description: The number of upvotes the comment has received.

accepted_by_question_poser.description: Timestamp indicating whether the solution has been accepted by the question poser (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

content_updated.description: Timestamp indicating when the solution's content was last updated (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

created.description: Timestamp indicating when the solution was created (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

description.description: This is the main text of the solution that provides the answer or guidance to the question posed.

expires.description: Timestamp indicating when the solution is about to expiry and requires revalidation (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

is_published.description: The solution is_published flag indicates whether the solution is published or not. A published solution is visible to all users.

published.description: Timestamp indicating when the solution was published (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

rating_count.description: Total number of ratings for the solution.

updated.description: Last time the resource has been updated in the DB. This date does not reflect content updates but any update to any fields of the record. Use content_updated for information about content updates.

upvote_count.description: The total number of upvotes the solution has received.

user.description: The user who created the solution

validity_period.description: Validity period until the solution is considered expired

revalidatedAt.description: The date when the solution was revalidated

validityPeriod.description: The validity period until the solution is considered expired

user.description: The user who validated the solution

SolutionValidation.description: solution validation object containing information about the validation status of the solution

approval_date.description: Date of the solution authority approval (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

UserId.description: The unique user identifier (UUID) used to reference a user across the Starmind platform.

about.description: Custom text entered by the user to describe themselves in their 'About Me' section
about.example: Experienced software developer with a passion for knowledge sharing.

avatar_url.description: URL to the user's avatar image

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g. ch, us, de) of the user

created.description: Timestamp indicating when the user was created (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

deleted.description: Timestamp indicating when the user was deleted (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ). Without admin permissions, only non deleted user will be returned

first_seen.description: Timestamp indicating when the user logged in the first time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

is_federated.description: Indicates whether the user authenticates using local credentials (email/password) rather than an external SSO provider (SAML, OpenID Connect)

is_technical.description: Indicates whether the user is a technical API user (non-interactive/machine account); when true the user typically authenticates via API key

automatic_new_user_assignment.description: Indicates if new users are automatically assigned

created.description: Timestamp indicating when the knowledge space was created (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.fffZ)

is_closed.description: Indicates if the knowledge space is non public

updated.description: Last time the resource has been updated in the DB.

KnowledgeSpace.description: The knowledge space object containing information about the knowledge space

Language.description: ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (e.g., 'en') representing a supported language. 'xx' can be used to indicate an unknown language.

ExpertConceptScores.description: Expert search concept matches including scores.

labels_for_languages.description: All available labels for different languages

description.example: open-source software for deploying and running of containerized applications

errors.description: Optional array of GraphQL errors; only present if errors occurred

UserId.description: The unique global user identifier (UUID) as path parameter used to reference the user across the Starmind platform.
UserId.name: id

x-ms-openai-manifest.modelDescription: Search for questions and answers (Q&A) in the internal Starmind knowledge network. This data source contains highly trustworthy solutions authored by subject matter experts. By default, results are sorted by relevance. The data source provided by Starmind is meticulously verified by experts for reliability and continuously updated to ensure accuracy and timeliness. Answers are rated from 1 to 5 stars, with 4 to 5 stars indicating a very high-quality solution. In addition, access the Starmind knowledge engine to find experts based on their expertise and contributions. The Starmind Connector enables seamless integration with Starmind's knowledge engine, allowing users to connect with experts across their organisation.
